iti82:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti82:2.1: “Monks, these three divine sounds sound forth among the devas on appropriate occasions.
iti82:2.3:
When a disciple of the noble ones, shaving off his hair & beard, clothing himself in the ochre robe,
makes up his mind to go forth from the home life into homelessness,
on that occasion the divine sound sounds forth among the devas:
iti82:2.4: ‘This disciple of the noble ones has made up his mind to do battle with Māra.’
iti82:2.5: This is the first divine sound that sounds forth among the devas on appropriate occasions.
iti82:3.1:
“When a disciple of the noble ones lives devoted to developing
the seven [sets of] qualities that are wings to awakening,
on that occasion the divine sound sounds forth among the devas:
iti82:3.2: ‘This disciple of the noble ones is doing battle with Māra.’
iti82:3.3: This is the second divine sound that sounds forth among the devas on appropriate occasions.
iti82:4.1:
“When a disciple of the noble ones, through the ending of effluents,
enters & remains in the effluent-free awareness-release & discernment-release,
directly knowing & realizing it for himself right in the here-&-now,
on that occasion the divine sound sounds forth among the devas:
iti82:4.2: ‘This disciple of the noble ones, having won the battle, now rules victorious at the head of the battle.’
iti82:4.3: This is the third divine sound that sounds forth among the devas on appropriate occasions.
iti82:4.4: “These are the three divine sounds that sound forth among the devas on appropriate occasions.”
iti82:5.2: –the disciple of the Rightly Self-awakened One–
iti82:5.4: to this great one, thoroughly mature.
iti82:6.2: you who have won the hard victory,
iti82:7.1: Thus they pay homage, the devas,
iti82:7.2: to one who has reached the heart’s goal,
iti82:7.3: for they see in him no means
iti82:7.4: that would bring him under Death’s sway.
